name: Flipt Feature Flag API
what_it_does: Open-source, self-hosted feature flag and experimentation platform with REST and gRPC APIs. Go-based server with GitOps support for declarative flag management and multi-namespace organization.
use_cases: Managing feature flags on-premises with full data sovereignty
GitOps-based flag management using YAML configuration in version control
Building feature flag workflows into CI/CD pipelines with REST/gRPC API
A/B testing and percentage rollouts for self-hosted infrastructure
Multi-namespace flag organization for microservices and team isolation
not_for: Organizations needing a managed SaaS feature flag service without operational overhead
Teams requiring third-party integrations and analytics dashboards out of the box
Non-technical operators who need a simple UI-only feature flag tool
best_when: An agent manages feature flags in a privacy-first, self-hosted environment with GitOps workflows and needs gRPC for performance-sensitive flag evaluation.
avoid_when: You want fully managed feature flags without self-hosting — use ConfigCat, LaunchDarkly, or Unleash Cloud.

auth.notes: Optional authentication (can run without auth for internal deployments). Static tokens or OIDC support when auth is enabled. Bootstrap token for initial setup. Namespace-level access control.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Apache 2.0: completely free, unlimited flags and namespaces
pricing.notes: Flipt is Apache 2.0 open-source. Flipt Cloud offers managed hosting with additional features. Self-hosted is fully functional with no limits. Strong GitOps and Kubernetes integration.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no rate limiting; performance depends on deployment

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST and gRPC APIs well-documented. Both APIs are feature-complete. Error responses follow gRPC status codes for gRPC API and HTTP status codes for REST. GitOps file format is documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Flag creation and updates are fully idempotent. GitOps declarative mode is inherently idempotent. gRPC unary RPCs are safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: gRPC API requires proto files or reflection — higher setup cost than REST for simple agents
Auth is optional by default — agents must verify auth is configured in production
GitOps mode vs API mode: changes via API don't create git commits automatically
Namespace isolation means agents must track namespace context for multi-team deployments

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open-source — fully auditable Go codebase. OIDC integration for enterprise auth. TLS for gRPC and REST. Self-hosted means no data leaves your infrastructure. SBOM available.
